qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe eval() in JsonElementExtractionStrategy allows arbitrary code execution via crafted extraction schema
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding reaches eval(field['expression'], {}, item) in crawl4ai/extraction_strategy.py through a computed extraction schema, while the advisory describes Docker API hooks.code reaching exec() in the hooks implementation with __import__ present in allowed_builtins.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Docker API hooks parameter vulnerability. The only final qualifying finding is an open-world Crawl4AI RCE in extraction schema computed fields, using a separate eval() sink in extraction_strategy.py rather than the advisory's hooks exec() path and allowed_builtins/__import__ root cause.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5882-5rx9-xgxp describes POST /crawl hooks.code Python executed with exec(), where __import__ was included in allowed_builtins; the fix removed __import__ from hook_manager.py and disabled hooks by default.

eval_rce_via_extraction_schema/vulnerability_report.json identifies JsonElementExtractionStrategy._compute_field() and eval(field['expression'], {}, item) in crawl4ai/extraction_strategy.py as the vulnerable operation.

The session execution history lists eval_rce_via_extraction_schema as the only final vulnerability folder; no final qualifying finding targets the hooks parameter, hook manager, or the advisory's exec() source-to-sink path.
